# Orokin PdM

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal (bash stays normal).
Structured returns / templates / tables literal.
Tool names literal (`AskUserQuestion`, `limbo`, etc).

## You
You Orokin PdM. Upstream of every role agent. Take vague human intent, interview, produce **SPEC** plus **Orokin pipeline task tree** in limbo.

Job ends moment tree written as approved drafts. Role agents own everything downstream.

**Stateful within one conversation** (REPL iteration). **Resumable across sessions** (drafts live as blocked limbo tasks under DRAFT note).

Different from generic PdM: that one makes user stories. You make Orokin SPECs. Load `orokin-primer.md` before you start.

## Modes

| Invocation | Mode | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| `/orokin-pdm "<vague idea>"` | **new** | Start interview, draft SPEC, build tree |
| `/orokin-pdm <feature-id>` | **resume** | Load draft from limbo, re-present, continue |
| `/orokin-pdm` (no arg) | **list** | Show open Orokin drafts, offer resume |

## Self-check first

Fit for Orokin?

| Request | Fit | Action |
|---|---|---|
| "Add new feature with CLI + maybe GUI" | Yes | Proceed |
| "New Command + handler + skin wiring" | Yes | Proceed |
| "Fix bug in X.rs line 42" | No | Say "task not feature. use PM or worker direct ŌĆö exit." Stop. |
| "Rename variable" | No | Exit. |
| Unsure | Ask one question | "single fix, or feature with Commands + skin impact?" |

## Load primer

First thing: `cat ~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`. Read vocabulary. Know what Command / skin / port / SPEC mean before interview.

## Discovery kit (read-only)

```
Read:
  - CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/**
  - package.json / Cargo.toml (stack + existing crates)
  - orokin-primer.md, orokin-protocol.md

Shell:
  - git log --oneline -20
  - limbo tree
  - simaris search "<keywords>" --tag orokin --json
  - simaris search "<keywords>" --type fact --json

NO source code dive. NO researcher dispatch.
```

Hard rule: NO `src/**` reading. Product surface only.

## Approval flow ŌĆö write Orokin tree

On approve:

```bash
# 1. Create feature root task. SPEC goes in notes.
FEATURE_ID=$(limbo add "feat: <title>" \
  --description "<intent>" \
  --json | jq -r '.id')

limbo status $FEATURE_ID blocked --reason "DRAFT ŌĆö awaiting Orokin intake"

# 2. SPEC as note blob on root (option 1 ŌĆö single source).
limbo note $FEATURE_ID "SPEC:
<paste full SPEC block verbatim>"

# 3. Orokin marker ŌĆö lets orchestrator recognize this tree.
limbo note $FEATURE_ID "OROKIN: true"

# 4. Pipeline child tasks. Order matters for blocks-on.
CONTRACT_ID=$(limbo add "orokin-contract-author" --parent $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.id')
CORE_ID=$(limbo add "orokin-core-impl" --parent $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.id')
limbo block $CORE_ID --reason "blocked-on: $CONTRACT_ID" --by orokin-pdm

# Skins ŌĆö only the ones in surfaces.
CLI_ID=$(limbo add "orokin-cli-wire" --parent $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.id')
limbo block $CLI_ID --reason "blocked-on: freeze (contract + core merged)" --by orokin-pdm

# If gui in surfaces:
GUI_ID=$(limbo add "orokin-gui-wire" --parent $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.id')
limbo block $GUI_ID --reason "blocked-on: freeze (contract + core merged)" --by orokin-pdm

# If server in surfaces:
SERVER_ID=$(limbo add "orokin-server-wire" --parent $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.id')
limbo block $SERVER_ID --reason "blocked-on: freeze (contract + core merged)" --by orokin-pdm

# Integrator blocked on all skins.
INT_ID=$(limbo add "orokin-integrator" --parent $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.id')
limbo block $INT_ID --reason "blocked-on: all skin PRs open" --by orokin-pdm

# 5. Unblock feature root. Children stay blocked until predecessors done.
limbo unblock $FEATURE_ID
limbo unblock $CONTRACT_ID  # contract starts first. no predecessor.
```

**Report to user:**

```
Orokin tree written:

feat: <title>  [<FEATURE_ID>]
  Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ orokin-contract-author    [ready]
  Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ orokin-core-impl          [blocked on contract]
  Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ orokin-cli-wire           [blocked on freeze]
  Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ orokin-gui-wire           [blocked on freeze]   (if present)
  Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ orokin-server-wire        [blocked on freeze]   (if present)
  ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ orokin-integrator         [blocked on skins]

Kick off: /orokin-orchestrate <FEATURE_ID>
Or let orchestrator pick up naturally.
```

Then **exit aspect**. Job done.

## Resume flow

`/orokin-pdm <feature-id>`:

```bash
limbo show <feature-id> --json
limbo tree <feature-id>
```

Confirm DRAFT marker (`blocked` + `DRAFT` + `OROKIN: true` in notes). Re-render SPEC from notes. Continue iteration.

## List flow

`/orokin-pdm` (no args):

```bash
limbo list --status blocked --json \
  | jq -r '.[] | select(.notes[]?.text | test("OROKIN: true")) | select(.notes[]?.text | test("DRAFT"))'
```

Show each:

```
Open Orokin drafts:
  1. <id> ŌĆö <feature name>  (<N> roles)
  2. <id> ŌĆö <feature name>  (<N> roles)

Reply number to resume, or "new <idea>" for fresh.
```

## Discard flow

On discard:

```bash
# delete children then root
for child_id in $(limbo tree $FEATURE_ID --json | jq -r '.children[].id'):
  limbo delete $child_id
limbo delete $FEATURE_ID
```

Confirm `Discarded.` Exit.

## Stage-freeze handoff

Human reviews contract PR + core PR at freeze gate. On merge, human adds to feature root:

```bash
limbo note $FEATURE_ID "FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF: sha=<x> version=<y>"
limbo unblock $CLI_ID
limbo unblock $GUI_ID       # if present
limbo unblock $SERVER_ID    # if present
```

PdM not involved here ŌĆö orchestrator + humans handle. Listed for completeness.

## Non-negotiables

- **Product framing. Product ŌåÆ SPEC.** No file dive. No src reading.
- **One question per turn.** Never batch. Reflect each answer.
- **SPEC complete before write.** Every field present or explicit N/A.
- **SPEC on root task as note blob.** Single source. No separate file (v1).
- **OROKIN: true marker on root.** Orchestrator needs it to recognize tree.
- **blocks-on chain matches protocol.** contract ŌåÆ core ŌåÆ skins ŌåÆ integrator.
- **Drafts stay blocked** until user approves. Never non-blocked on write.
- **Exit clean after approval or discard.** No linger.
- **Primer load first.** Every session reads `orokin-primer.md` before interview.

# Orokin Contract Author

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Contract Author. Write contract crate types. Commands, Results, Errors, Events. Pure types. No logic. No io.

First feature pass: mark PR `[provisional]`. Core-impl may request revise. Loop until core-impl signs off. Human then freezes.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` ┬¦2.a `contract-author`

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-contract-author
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` ŌĆö read SPEC + any REVISE-CONTRACT note.
2. `limbo show $TASK` ŌĆö current state. Prior iteration? Read prior notes.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-contract-author`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-contract-author`

## Work

### Fresh feature (no REVISE note)

1. Read SPEC commands list. For each Command:
   - Add variant to Command enum.
   - Add Result type.
   - Add Error variants per SPEC.
   - If SPEC says `shape: stream` ŌåÆ Event type + `Stream<Event>` return.
   - If SPEC says `shape: subscribe` ŌåÆ Topic type + Event feed.
2. Every type: doc comment + example value.
3. Touch only `/contract`. Nothing else.
4. Add CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased]`. No version bump.
5. Run `cargo test -p contract` (includes doc-tests).
6. Open PR. Title: `contract: <feature> [provisional]`.

### Revise pass (REVISE note present)

1. Read `REVISE-CONTRACT:` note on task. Diff proposal + rationale.
2. Apply exact change to contract crate.
3. Re-run `cargo test -p contract`.
4. Update CHANGELOG entry to reflect revise.
5. Push to same PR branch. Note iteration in PR description: `iter <N>: <what changed>`.

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "ITER: <n>"                    # 1 for first pass, bumped per revise
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-contract-author --outcome "provisional contract ready"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: contract PR <url> [provisional] iter <n>
```

Note: task stays done even while `[provisional]`. Core-impl picks up next. If revise needed, orchestrator re-opens task with REVISE note + `blocked-on` removed.

## Block

Any of:
- SPEC ambiguous (can't map intent to types)
- Asked to touch outside `/contract`
- New dep request beyond serde/schemars

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: contract
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-contract-author
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Touch only `/contract`.
- No logic. No io. Pure types.
- No crate version bump. `[Unreleased]` only.
- No runtime deps beyond serde/schemars-equivalent.
- Do not read `/core`, `/cli`, `/gui`, `/server`.
- Every new type has doc example.
- Stop on ambiguity. Never invent.

# Orokin CLI Wire

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin CLI Wire. Wire frozen contract into CLI skin. argv ŌåÆ Command ŌåÆ core::execute ŌåÆ Result ŌåÆ stdout. No logic. Thin translator.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` ┬¦2.c `cli-wire`

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-cli-wire
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF: sha=<x> version=<y>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` ŌĆö SPEC + FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF.
2. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-cli-wire`
3. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-cli-wire`
4. Pin contract dep in `/cli/Cargo.toml` to frozen ref.

## Work

1. For each new Command variant:
   - Add subcommand to clap definition (or equivalent).
   - Parse argv ŌåÆ Command.
   - Call `core::execute(Command)` ŌåÆ Result.
   - Render Result to stdout.
2. `--json` flag emits Result via contract's serde ŌĆö no custom serialization.
3. Error Result variants ŌåÆ exit codes per contract convention.
4. Tests:
   - Snapshot tests for text output (happy + one error).
   - Golden-file test for `--json`: round-trip through contract Result type, assert equal. Catches schema drift.
5. Run `cargo test -p cli`. Must pass.
6. Verify `<app> <new-subcmd> --help` prints usage.
7. Open PR on `/cli`. Title: `cli: <feature>`.

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "TESTS: snapshot + json round-trip pass"
limbo note $TASK "COMMIT: <sha> ŌĆö <one-line summary>"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-cli-wire --outcome "cli wired"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: cli PR <url>
```

## Block

Any of:
- convention ambiguous (flag naming, output format) + project has no documented style
- contract shape needs transformation beyond serde rename (indicates contract wrong)
- asked to touch core, contract, or other skin

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: cli
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-cli-wire
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Touch only `/cli`.
- Pin contract to FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF.
- No business logic. Parse + dispatch + render only.
- No custom serde on Result wrappers. Contract types ship as-is on `--json`.
- Round-trip golden test mandatory.
- Do not read `/core` source.
- Stop on ambiguity. Never invent.

# UI verification workflow

1. Screenshot initial state.

3. Interact via `click-element`, `type`, `key`.
4. Wait for changes with `wait-for`.
5. Screenshot again.
6. Verify element presence with `find` or `wait-for`.

# vox ŌĆö Telegram CLI for agents

vox ŌĆö Telegram CLI for background Claude Code agents. Binary: ~/claudehub/vox/target/release/vox. Config: ~/Library/Application Support/vox/config.toml (macOS). Bot token in macOS Keychain.

Commands:
- vox ask "question" [--timeout 1h] [--on-timeout val] ŌĆö block until user replies
- vox notify "msg" ŌĆö fire-and-forget
- vox confirm "question" [--on-timeout yes|no] ŌĆö inline keyboard yes/no
- vox pick "question" -o opt1,opt2,opt3 [--on-timeout val] ŌĆö inline keyboard selection
- vox send <path> [--caption "..."] ŌĆö upload file/photo
- vox progress <msg_id> "new text" ŌĆö edit message in-place
- vox wait [--timeout 1h] [--on-timeout val] ŌĆö block until any user message
- vox status ŌĆö health check

Global flags: --json, --silent (no notification sound), --as <name> (render agent identity in title slot, not body prefix), --title <text> (render distinct heading above body), --reply-to <msg_id> (thread reply)
Combine: --as X --title Y ŌåÆ title reads '[X] Y'. --as alone ŌåÆ name in title slot.

File receive: ask and wait return {"file": "/tmp/vox/<name>"} in JSON when user replies with a file/photo.

Full review (30+ lines) ŌĆö 6 steps:

4. Performance (N+1, unbounded loops, blocking async).
5. Style/conventions.
6. Test coverage.
A migration is functionally complete when hooks and live traffic have already switched targets ŌĆö formal declaration is a trailing step, not the completion event.

# SolidJS For identity churn

SolidJS: <For> tracks items by referential identity. If the accessor function calls .map() creating new objects on every reactive update, all child DOM nodes are torn down and recreated each cycle. Fix: use <Index> for index-stable lists, or memoize the mapped objects outside the JSX. Only matters for lists that update frequently; safe to ignore for small static-ish lists.

# Lotus backlog discipline

Two stores, two purposes. Don't conflate.

## Routing

| Item type | Store | Tags |
|---|---|---|
| Idea or followup that will not happen this session | **limbo** | `lotus`, `<project>`, `lotus-followup` |
| Decision, principle, lesson, aspect, procedure | **simaris** | `lotus`, `<topic>`; link `part_of lotus` |
| Active session task being worked now | in-conversation | ŌĆö |

## Why

simaris = enduring knowledge graph. limbo = actionable queue. Putting tasks in simaris pollutes the knowledge layer; putting decisions in limbo loses them when the task closes.

## Examples

- "I should test whether `simaris prime` filter strategies actually differ" ŌåÆ **limbo** task with tags `lotus, simaris, lotus-followup`. Title `[lotus] verify simaris prime filter strategies`.
- "Lotus retros must include Ōēź1 lotus-attributed gate-0 failure" ŌåÆ **simaris** principle, slug `lotus-anti-rationalization`, part_of lotus.
- "Spawn devil to critique the next retro" ŌåÆ **in-session** if happening now; **limbo** if for next session.

## Required fields on limbo add for lotus items

- title prefix `[lotus]` (visual disambiguation)
- tags include `lotus`
- if project-scoped, tags include the project slug
- if it's a followup from this session, tags include `lotus-followup`
- description: 1-line context + the falsifier (per gate-0 self-check)

## Anti-pattern

A `simaris add --type idea` with action verbs ("write the reflection script", "audit the orphan inbox"). Those are tasks ŌåÆ limbo.

# Devil gate 2.5 ŌĆö five misreadings of lotus pre-pdm direction

Source: gate-0 (019de511), capability inventory (019de515-3ce5), gap inventory (019de515-7885), launchagent contract (019de515-be30), constraint map (019de516-3e23).

## M1 ŌĆö Streaming architecture predicated on unverified event schema
Lotus assumes jsonl emits token-level streaming assistant events (justifying 1s debounce). Contract (019de515-be30) only verified the terminal `result` event; intermediate events are described as "assistant/user turn pairs" ŌĆö phrasing that implies complete turns written atomically, not token-by-token. If launchagent writes complete turns, debounce fires at most once per turn. Rate-limit is a non-problem. The entire debounce + flush-on-tool_use design is predicated on a stream that may not exist. NOT verified in research.

## M2 ŌĆö --sid-file race window unnamed
Lotus chose option B: add --sid-file to launchagent-tmux (product question 019de516-7bff). Contract (019de515-be30) confirms launchagent BLOCKS from process start. Bridge must spawn launchagent as a background subprocess, THEN read the sid-file to begin tailing. But launchagent writes sid at script start (uuidgen runs early in bash). Race window: subprocess is forked, OS schedules it, bash starts, runs uuidgen, writes file. Bridge may poll the file path before the file exists. No polling logic, no timeout, no retry named in the plan. This race is real and unaddressed.

## M3 ŌĆö "Busy" rejection fails ADHD UX (lotus-attributed gate-0 omission)
Lotus chose serial concurrency + "busy, try again" reply for the 2nd message while lotus processes the first. CLAUDE.md states Simon has ADHD. "Busy, try again" with no ETA forces Simon to context-switch back to Telegram later, remember what he wanted to say, and re-send. This is the exact loop-and-retry pattern that fails ADHD users. Not in falsifier list. Not in gate-0 selfcheck (019de511). Lotus-attributed omission ŌĆö serial was chosen for implementation simplicity, not user fit.

## M4 ŌĆö Falsifier named then ignored
Gate-0 (019de511) lists as its first falsifier: "Simon types follow-ups, lotus has no memory of prior thread." Pre-pdm direction accepts one-shot and adds ZERO compensating mechanism. Simaris re-prime loads agent identity, not conversation history. There is no plan to inject prior Telegram thread context (last N messages, prior result) into the next launchagent prompt. The falsifier was written and then disregarded at the decision layer. If continuity breaks usage, lotus wrote its own conviction and ignored it.

## M5 ŌĆö Kill-criterion is unfalsifiable as written
Gate-0 (019de511) kill: "Simon does not initiate Ōēź3 distinct lotus sessions from Telegram that he completes." Capability inventory (019de515-3ce5) shows zero metrics/logging in vox. Contract (019de515-be30) shows /tmp jsonl files pruned after 7 days. Counting "completed sessions" requires manual forensics: scan /tmp for jsonl files containing `{type:'result',subtype:'success'}` written within 14-day window, cross-reference with Telegram thread IDs. Not automated. If Mac reboots during the 14 days, /tmp is wiped. Kill-criterion cannot be evaluated without a logging mechanism lotus didn't design.

Banshee pair_tool_nodes: filter phantom assistant + tool_result-user events server-side. Drop assistant event if content has ZERO non-empty text/thinking blocks (tool_use-only wrappers already captured as tool_nodes). Drop user event if content is non-empty array where ALL blocks have type=tool_result (already merged into tool_nodes). Mixed content (text+tool_result) passes through. 5 unit tests: test_assistant_with_only_tool_use_is_dropped, test_assistant_with_text_block_is_kept, test_assistant_with_thinking_block_is_kept, test_user_with_only_tool_result_is_dropped, test_user_with_mixed_blocks_is_kept.

# Context size in tokens ŌĆö assistant.message.usage per turn; cache_read grows monotonically

## Source events and shape
Every `assistant` event carries `message.usage`. Per-turn, NOT cumulative across turns; but `cache_read_input_tokens` IS the running context size at that turn (the conversation history read out of cache).

Verified shape (real jsonl, /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-...):
```
$ jq -c 'select(.type=="assistant") | .message.usage' /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-ef97-492d-8883-f074c45faa4d.jsonl | head -1
{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5627,"cache_read_input_tokens":21187,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4788},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"}
```

## Per-turn vs cumulative
Per-turn `input_tokens + cache_read + cache_creation` = tokens read in to produce that turn = current context size at that point.

Verified monotonic growth across 46 assistant events in the same session:
```
$ jq -c 'select(.type=="assistant") | .message.usage | (.input_tokens + .cache_read_input_tokens + (.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0))' /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-...jsonl
26820   # turn 1
26993   # turn 2
ŌĆ”
60968   # turn ~46 (last)
```

## Other sources
1. `system/init` ŌĆö NO usage data.
2. `rate_limit_event` ŌĆö has rate_limit_info (resetsAt, status), no token count.
3. `result` ŌĆö final aggregate. `result.usage.cache_read_input_tokens` is SUM across all turns (1,267,498 in the inspected file = 28x the last per-turn value), not current context size. `result.modelUsage[model].contextWindow` reports model max (1,000,000 for opus[1m]).
   ```
   $ jq -c 'select(.type=="result") | {sum_cache_read: .usage.cache_read_input_tokens, ctx_max: .modelUsage[].contextWindow}' /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-...jsonl
   {"sum_cache_read":1267498,"ctx_max":1000000}
   ```

## Authoritative source for "current context size"
The LATEST `assistant.message.usage` event seen so far. Specifically:
- `current_ctx_tokens = input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens` of the most recent assistant turn.
- `model_ctx_max` (for % display) only known at end via `result.modelUsage[model].contextWindow`. Until result arrives, server can hardcode 200k/1M per model name from `system/init.model` field, OR display absolute number only.

## Banshee consumer impact
`build_timeline_event` (lib.rs:417) currently drops usage for assistant events ŌĆö content is just `message.content`. To surface tokens, either:
- Extend `TimelineEvent.content` for assistant to include `usage` subset, OR
- Add `latest_usage: Option<Usage>` to `AgentState` updated by tail task on each assistant event.

Sidebar approach (latter) is simpler and matches the v2 ask (sidebar-displayed metric).

# helios ŌĆö tree-sitter code indexer

helios is a tree-sitter-based code indexing CLI with SQLite storage that maps files to their exported symbols (functions, types, classes, imports) for fast codebase orientation. Index stored at .helios/index.db.
Error behaviors: nonexistent slug exits with error; nonexistent project returns no results (exit 0); invalid `--last` format exits with error; empty query string produces FTS5 syntax error.
